title: Perishable Goods
description: Perishable Goods — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Perishable Goods" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "Adèle Pleydell"
memes.description: "The youngest of the Pleydell sisters and wife of Captain 'Boy' Pleydell, whose kidnapping by Rose Noble's gang in Carinthia drives the entire plot of the novel. Her courage, wit, and eventual love for Jonathan Mansel make her the emotional centre of the story."

memes.label: "Adèle Rescued"
memes.description: "The successful rescue of Adèle Pleydell from the Castle of Gath—a moment of extraordinary daring in which Mansel seizes an unguarded instant on the terrace to pull her to safety, and Adèle, safe at last, declares her love."

memes.label: "Adèle's Signals"
memes.description: "Adèle signals from the southwest tower of the Castle of Gath, and Mansel uses a mirror flash from a distant mountain peak to confirm his presence and learn where she is lodged."

memes.label: "Adele Tends Mansel"
memes.description: "Adele asks Tester's permission to tend Mansel's wounds. Her bond with Mansel deepens as she cares for him."

memes.label: Adèle's Glove
memes.description: A single riding glove belonging to Adèle Pleydell, found on the road by the servant Fitch — the first physical clue in the investigation of her kidnapping and the anchor point from which Jonathan Mansel traced her abductors' route to the Castle of Gath.

memes.label: "Austria"
memes.description: "The country in central Europe where Adèle Pleydell is held captive and where the rescue operation takes place across the Carinthian mountains."

memes.label: "The King's Bedchamber"
memes.description: "The King's Bedchamber within the Castle of Gath — a fine chamber entered via the King's Closet, where the party takes shelter and where Mansel later recovers from his wounds."

memes.label: "Beechwoods Reconnaissance"
memes.description: "Mansel and Bell reconnoiter the beechwoods at the foot of the Castle of Gath's cliff, scouting for enemy activity and securing the area."

memes.label: "Bell and Carson Reconnaissance"
memes.description: "The reconnaissance mission by Bell and Carson to scout the Castle of Gath's surroundings — an intelligence-gathering operation that provides vital tactical data for the rescue party."

memes.label: "Bicycle Diversion"
memes.description: "Bell crashes a bicycle into Casemate's car as a diversion during the Castle of Gath surveillance operation in Lass, allowing Carson to pass a covert message to Mansel about 'a broken spring.'"

memes.label: The Bookseller of Lass
memes.description: The bookseller of Lass is a "respectable citizen" of the old Carinthian town who provides [[jonathan-mansel|Jonathan Mansel]]'s party with crucial intelligence about the [[castle-of-gath|Castle of Gath]], including a detailed written guide to its secret passages and defenses.

memes.label: "Dr. Buchinger"
memes.description: "An Innsbruck surgeon who performs the emergency operation on Jonathan Mansel after the final confrontation, working alongside Dr. Rachel to save Mansel's life."

memes.label: "Buchinger's Emergency Surgery"
memes.description: "Dr. Buchinger performs emergency surgery on Mansel at the Castle of Gath. Rachel assists. The tense three-day wait for Mansel to survive."

memes.label: "Captain Pleydell"
memes.description: "Adèle Pleydell's husband, known as 'Boy,' who is laid up with a broken leg throughout the novel and lies in a chamber at the Pleydell farm while Mansel's party rescues his wife."

memes.label: Carinthia
memes.description: "Carinthia — a region in the Austrian Alps — is the destination that figures prominently in the plot of Perishable Goods, serving as both a vacation spot and a key to the mystery."

memes.label: "Carinthia Kidnapping"
memes.description: "The abduction of Adèle Pleydell in Carinthia, Austria, by agents of Rose Noble. This event is the inciting incident of Perishable Goods, triggering Mansel's expedition to recover her."

memes.label: Carson
memes.description: "Carson is Jonathan Mansel's loyal body-servant who appears throughout Perishable Goods, from the discovery of the burglary in Cleveland Row to the final operation at the Castle of Gath."

memes.label: "Carson and Bell Reconnaissance"
memes.description: "Carson and Bell conduct surveillance operations around the Castle of Gath, observing enemy movements, guarding approach routes, and supporting Mansel's strategic deception of Rose Noble."

memes.label: "Casemate Interrogation"
memes.description: "Jonathan Mansel questions Casemate about the layout of the Castle of Gath and Adèle Pleydell's whereabouts after capturing him during the reconnaissance operation."

memes.label: "Castle of Gath Discovered"
memes.description: "Following Rouse's trail to Lass, Mansel's party identifies the Castle of Gath as Adèle's prison. Chandos rides on the roof of Rose Noble's car to discover the castle, then Mansel sights Adèle on the terrace and signals her with a watch-case mirror, establishing contact and mapping the fortress."

memes.label: "Castle of Gath Operation"
memes.description: "The extended operation by Jonathan Mansel's party to infiltrate the Castle of Gath in Carinthia, rescue Adèle Pleydell from Rose Noble's captivity, and dismantle Noble's criminal network. Spans Chapters IV through IX of Perishable Goods."

memes.label: "Chandos's Flat"
memes.description: "William Chandos's London residence, the starting point of his journey to Cleveland Row where the novel's events are set in motion."

memes.label: "Chandos's Narrative Voice"
memes.description: "The distinctive first-person narrative voice of William Chandos — gentleman, observer, and loyal friend — through whose eyes the entire story of Perishable Goods is told."

memes.label: "Channel Crossing"
memes.description: "The rescue party crosses the English Channel from Newhaven to Dieppe. Bell expedites customs clearance with bribes. A planted note warning 'The goods are perishable' is discovered on the car seat, proving Rose Noble's agents are tracking every move."

memes.label: "I — First Blood"
memes.description: "It was in October, 1926, that George Hanbury and I first set up house in Wiltshire; and, since for the next six months we hunted four days a week, yet would ..."

memes.label: "Chapter I: Scene Setting"
memes.description: "The opening passages of Perishable Goods that establish the narrator's world, introduce the key characters, and set the stage for the drama to come."

memes.label: "II — We Take the Field"
memes.description: "To show Mansel a memento so ugly went against the grain: yet we dared not suppress it, so I drove to where he sat waiting, and Hanbury gave the paper into hi..."

memes.label: "III — In Touch"
memes.description: "When we came in at nightfall, at the end of our second patrol, Rouse was still abroad in his car."

memes.label: "IV — The Castle of Gath"
memes.description: "There was no time to make any plan."

memes.label: "V — Mansel Takes Off the Gloves"
memes.description: "At ten o’clock the next morning Mansel, Carson and I visited the Castle of Gath."

memes.label: "VI — The Love of a Lady"
memes.description: "Never, I suppose were hopes lifted so high one moment and dashed so low the next."

memes.label: "VII — We Practise to Deceive"
memes.description: "Try as I will, I cannot distinctly remember what then took place, and I think that I acted blindly, as a man in a trance."

memes.label: "VIII — Out of Sight, Out of Mind"
memes.description: "The shutting of a door roused me, and I sat up to find myself alone."

memes.label: "IX — Full Measure"
memes.description: "I shall never forgive myself for forgetting that Casemate was still at large."

memes.label: Character Index
memes.description: Alphabetical index of all characters appearing in Dornford Yates' Perishable Goods (1928).

memes.label: "Cleveland Row Conference"
memes.description: "The council of war held in Jonathan Mansel's flat at Cleveland Row, where the rescue party gathers to plan their response to Adèle Pleydell's kidnapping — a scene of strategic deliberation that sets the entire operation in motion."

memes.label: "The Cleveland Row Council"
memes.description: "After the telegram reveals Adèle's abduction, Mansel convenes a council at Cleveland Row. He rejects paying the ransom, lays out the rescue strategy, and dispatches the team to prepare cars, arms, and logistics for the cross-continental pursuit of Rose Noble."

memes.label: Cleveland Square
memes.description: "Mansel's flat in Cleveland Square, London — the scene of the burglary that opens Perishable Goods and the base from which the expedition to Carinthia is launched."

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "Colophon"

memes.label: "The Customs Frontier"
memes.description: "The customs checkpoints at the French border that the rescue party must navigate — an obstacle overcome through advance preparation, bribery, and the resourcefulness of Bell and Rowley."

memes.label: Daphne Pleydell
memes.description: "Daphne Pleydell is a member of the Pleydell family and part of Mansel's social circle in Perishable Goods, present at White Ladies during the crisis over Adèle's kidnapping."

memes.label: "Dedication"
memes.description: "To Harrow School, as I remember it, when I had the honour to wear the Harrow Hat."

memes.label: "Dieppe"
memes.description: "The French Channel port where the rescue party lands after crossing from England, and a key transit point in both directions of their continental journey."

memes.label: "The Royal Dining-Room"
memes.description: "The Royal Dining-Room of the Castle of Gath, a grand chamber accessible through the King's Closet, which becomes a contested space during the rescue operation."

memes.label: Adèle's Dressing-Case
memes.description: Adèle Pleydell's dressing-case — a portable case packed with her personal belongings at Mansel's request, demanded by her kidnappers as part of the ransom conditions, and retrieved during her rescue from the Castle of Gath.

memes.label: England
memes.description: England serves as the home base and point of departure for [[jonathan-mansel|Jonathan Mansel]]'s expedition in Dornford Yates's *Perishable Goods* (1928). The novel opens in England and the characters' English identity shapes their perspective throughout the Continental adventure.

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: Chronological index of key events in Dornford Yates' Perishable Goods (1928).

memes.label: Field-Glasses
memes.description: The high-powered field-glasses (binoculars) carried by Jonathan Mansel and his party during the Carinthian campaign — essential optical instruments used for long-range surveillance, the first sighting of Adèle at the Castle of Gath, and the heliograph signalling that established contact with her.

memes.label: "Final Confrontation"
memes.description: "The climactic final confrontation of Perishable Goods in Chapter IX, 'Full Measure,' in which Jonathan Mansel is mortally wounded while completing the destruction of Rose Noble's network, and dies in the great bedchamber of the Castle of Gath attended by Adèle Pleydell."

memes.label: Fitch
memes.description: A servant employed at the Pleydell family's Carinthian farm who discovered Adèle Pleydell's glove on the road — the first tangible clue in tracing her abduction.

memes.label: "Folkestone"
memes.description: "The English Channel port from which Mansel's rescue party departs for continental Europe, marking the transition from the English to the Austrian phase of the novel."

memes.label: "France"
memes.description: "The country through which the rescue party travels en route to Austria, entering via Dieppe and crossing the French countryside at speed."

memes.label: "Fuel Tank Shot"
memes.description: "Bell shoots the enemy's fuel tank from a moving Rolls-Royce during the mountain pursuit of Punter and Bunch's car."

memes.label: George Hanbury
memes.description: "George Hanbury is the narrator's closest friend and a trusted ally in Jonathan Mansel's enterprises."

memes.label: "The Grave Digging"
memes.description: "Bell, Carson, and Rowley dig a grave in the mountain dell as part of Jonathan Mansel's psychological warfare against Jute—an act of theatrical menace that escalates into genuine execution when Jute produces Adèle's blouse."
aliases: the psychological warfare at the dell

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "Imprint"

memes.label: "The Narrative Arc of Perishable Goods"
memes.description: "An analysis of the causal chain of events that drives the plot of Perishable Goods — from the Cleveland Row burglary through the Castle of Gath operation to the final confrontation."

memes.label: "Person-Place Affinities in Perishable Goods"
memes.description: "An analysis of how major characters in Perishable Goods are bound to specific locations — Chandos to Cleveland Row, Mansel to White Ladies, Rose Noble to the Castle of Gath — and what these affinities reveal about identity and fate."

memes.label: Jonah
memes.description: Jonah is a central character in Dornford Yates's *Perishable Goods* (1928), addressed by this name throughout the novel. The name "Jonah" functions as a familiar diminutive, and the character is a principal actor in the Carinthia adventure and the Castle of Gath operation.

memes.label: Jonathan Mansel
memes.description: "Jonathan Mansel is the central protagonist of Perishable Goods — a master strategist who captains the group's daring enterprises against crime."

memes.label: "Mansel's Leadership"
memes.description: "An analysis of Jonathan Mansel's distinctive leadership style — combining military precision with personal loyalty, tactical brilliance with moral clarity, and absolute authority with earned trust."

memes.label: "Jute Confrontation"
memes.description: "A series of tense encounters with Jute, one of Rose Noble's enforcers, during the protagonists' reconnaissance around Castle of Gath in Chapters IV–V of Perishable Goods."

memes.label: "Jute's Intelligence Report"
memes.description: "Jute reports that Big Willie has been summoned to Lass, and Casemate briefs Rose Noble on the road to the Castle of Gath, revealing the intelligence network within Noble's organization."

memes.label: King Maximilian
memes.description: King Maximilian I (1459–1519), Holy Roman Emperor — invoked in Perishable Goods as the historical presence whose spirit haunts the Castle of Gath and whose epithet "the Last of the Knights" provides the chivalric frame through which Jonathan Mansel's death is understood.

memes.label: Ladder
memes.description: Custom wrought-iron one-pole ladders, forged by a village smith at [[jonathan-mansel|Jonathan Mansel]]'s direction, serve as essential climbing equipment during the [[castle-of-gath-operation|Castle of Gath operation]] in Dornford Yates's *Perishable Goods* (1928).

memes.label: Lass
memes.description: "An ancient, curious maze-like town in the mountains of Carinthia where the Three Kings Hotel serves as the group's staging point for the Castle of Gath operation."

memes.label: Location Index
memes.description: Gazetteer of all locations appearing in Dornford Yates' Perishable Goods (1928).

memes.label: "London"
memes.description: "The capital city of England, where Jonathan Mansel's flat in Cleveland Row is located and where much of the story's planning and aftermath takes place."

memes.label: "Major Pleydell"
memes.description: "Head of the Pleydell family and owner of the White Ladies estate in the New Forest, who joins Mansel's rescue party in Austria after his sister Adèle is kidnapped."

memes.label: "Mansel's Household"
memes.description: "The household staff serving Jonathan Mansel — body-servant Carson, manservant Bell, and Hanbury's servant Rowley — who provide essential operational support throughout the novel's adventures."

memes.label: "Mansel's War Record"
memes.description: "Jonathan Mansel's distinguished military service in the Great War, the source of his tactical brilliance, his limp from a war wound, and the reputation that makes Rose Noble fear and respect him."

memes.label: "Mansel Wounded"
memes.description: "Jonathan Mansel is shot in the stomach by Casemate during the final confrontation; his life hangs in the balance."

memes.label: Mansel's Limp
memes.description: Jonathan Mansel's limp — the visible legacy of a wound sustained in the Great War — a physical characteristic that humanises the otherwise superhuman protagonist, marks his body with the cost of service, and creates a poignant contrast with his extraordinary physical capabilities during the rescue campaign.

memes.label: "Mansel's Pledge"
memes.description: "The solemn pledge given by Jonathan Mansel to Rose Noble — a gentleman's word of honour that binds him, even to his enemy, and shapes the moral framework of the novel's climax."

memes.label: Marlborough Gate
memes.description: Marlborough Gate — the gateway in St. James's, London, through which William Chandos passed while turning into Cleveland Row on the morning the novel's action begins, locating the story's opening within the precise geography of gentleman's London.

memes.label: "Marlborough Street"
memes.description: "A London street invoked rhetorically in Perishable Goods as a symbol of civilized conduct during a confrontation in Chapter IV."

memes.label: "The Monstrance"
memes.description: "A religious artifact — a vessel used to display the consecrated Host — that hung on the wall of Jonathan Mansel's flat in Cleveland Row and was notably not stolen during Rose Noble's burglary."

memes.label: Most Referenced
memes.description: The most cross-referenced entries across the Perishable Goods wiki, ranked by total inbound links.

memes.label: "New Forest"
memes.description: "A historic woodland and heath region in Hampshire, England, and the setting of White Ladies, the Pleydell family estate and home of Jonathan Mansel in Perishable Goods."

memes.label: The Oratory
memes.description: The oratory at the Castle of Gath is a chamber adjacent to the chapel, featuring a casement window overlooking the courtyard, which serves as a refuge for George Hanbury and the servants during the final confrontation in Dornford Yates's Perishable Goods (1928).

memes.label: Organization Index
memes.description: Index of organizations, factions, and groups in Dornford Yates' Perishable Goods (1928).

memes.label: "Perishable Goods"
memes.description: '"Perishable goods" — the chilling phrase used by Rose Noble to describe the kidnapped Adèle Pleydell, giving the novel its title and encoding its central theme: the reduction of a human being to a commodity whose value depends on her remaining undamaged.'

memes.label: "Pleydell Family"
memes.description: "The Pleydell family of White Ladies in the New Forest — Major Pleydell, Daphne, Adèle, and her husband 'Boy' Pleydell — whose members are central to the novel's plot and whose Carinthian farm serves as a staging point for the Gath operation."

memes.label: "The Pleydell Farm"
memes.description: "The rented farm near St. Martin where the Pleydell family stays during their Austrian holiday, serving as the rescue party's initial destination and base of operations."

memes.label: "Poganec"
memes.description: "A village in Carinthia, Austria, associated with the Pleydell family. Serves as a strategic rest stop and base of operations for Mansel and his party throughout Perishable Goods."

memes.label: "Poganec Reconnaissance"
memes.description: "Mansel establishes a reconnaissance operation based from Poganec and Villach. The team searches the countryside west toward where Adèle was taken, using a hog's back as an observation point. Mansel identifies the kidnappers' observation post at a bridge above a waterfall."

memes.label: "Punter and Bunch Captured"
memes.description: "Punter and Bunch are captured at the final confrontation in the wood; they are covered by Bell while Mansel tends to Chandos."

memes.label: "Punter Chase"
memes.description: "The pursuit and attempted capture of Punter, one of Rose Noble's enforcers, by Mansel's party in Chapters VII–VIII of Perishable Goods."

memes.label: "The Queen's Rooms"
memes.description: "A suite of rooms within the Castle of Gath that the rescue party passes through while navigating the castle's interior passages during Adèle Pleydell's rescue."

memes.label: "Dr. Rachel"
memes.description: "A doctor from Lass who, together with Dr. Buchinger, performs emergency surgery on Jonathan Mansel after he is wounded in the final confrontation at the Castle of Gath."

memes.label: Rope
memes.description: Rope and cordage serve as essential climbing and signaling equipment during the [[castle-of-gath-operation|Castle of Gath operation]] in Dornford Yates's *Perishable Goods* (1928). [[jonathan-mansel|Jonathan Mansel]] procures rope from a local factory to enable silent ascent of the castle walls.

memes.label: Rose Noble
memes.description: "Rose Noble is the primary antagonist of Perishable Goods — the master criminal behind the theft of Mansel's papers and the central threat driving the plot."

memes.label: "Rose Noble Confronted"
memes.description: "Jonathan Mansel confronts Rose Noble in the royal Dining-room of the Castle of Gath for a high-stakes negotiation over Adèle Pleydell's fate—a verbal duel that escalates from cold bargaining to sudden violence."

memes.label: "Rose Noble's Death"
memes.description: "Rose Noble meets his end at Mansel's hand in the ambush in the wood. Bell and Carson dig his grave."

memes.label: "Rose Noble's Pursuit"
memes.description: "Rose Noble and his remaining gang pursue the rescue party after Adèle Pleydell's escape from the Castle of Gath—a high-speed chase through the Carinthian mountains that marks the beginning of the endgame."

memes.label: "Hannibal Rouse"
memes.description: "A fake clergyman and agent of Rose Noble stationed at Villach, whose clumsy surveillance of Mansel's party in Chapter III leads to a violent confrontation and his eventual flight."

memes.label: "Rouse Identified"
memes.description: "Rouse delivers a box of 'flowers' from 'Mr. Wilberforce' — actually a lock of Adèle's beautiful brown hair. Mansel reveals Rouse is Rose Noble's spy, having suspected him from the first encounter at the bridge. A trap is laid to follow Rouse to Adèle's location."

memes.label: "Rouse Infiltration"
memes.description: "Hannibal Rouse's infiltration as a fake clergyman in Villach, and Jonathan Mansel's counter-trap to expose him as an agent of Rose Noble, in Chapter III of Perishable Goods."

memes.label: "Rowley"
memes.description: "George Hanbury's old servant, a steadfast member of Mansel's party who assists in the Castle of Gath operation from Austria to the final confrontation."

memes.label: "The Salzburg Drive"
memes.description: "The high-speed cross-continental drive from Dieppe through France, Germany, and Austria to Carinthia. Mansel leads the two Rolls-Royces on a punishing thousand-mile journey, arriving at Poganec within twenty-four hours."

memes.label: "Sava"
memes.description: "A village in Carinthia, Austria, situated approximately ten miles from the main route. Connected by a bridge and road from St. Martin, Sava appears as a geographical reference point in the protagonists' operations around Castle of Gath."

memes.label: The Southeast Tower
memes.description: The southeast tower at the [[castle-of-gath|Castle of Gath]] is a section of the castle not shown on the visitor's tour, which serves as both the residence of the caretakers and a potential defensive position during the [[castle-of-gath-operation|Castle of Gath operation]] in Dornford Yates's *Perishable Goods* (1928).

memes.label: "The Spiral Staircase"
memes.description: "The spiral stone staircase within the Castle of Gath that connects the archway beneath the cascade to the King's Closet, serving as the rescue party's secret route into the heart of the fortress."

memes.label: "Arrival at St. Martin"
memes.description: "After the thousand-mile drive, Mansel's party arrives at the Pleydell farm in Poganec near St. Martin. They meet Major Pleydell, the white-haired Captain Pleydell, and Daphne Pleydell, and receive the first detailed account of Adèle's disappearance."

memes.label: Stable Yard
memes.description: Stable Yard — the location in St. James's, London, of the garage where Jonathan Mansel kept his Rolls-Royce and where the party's two cars were prepared, packed, and loaded by Carson on the eve of the European expedition to rescue Adèle Pleydell.

memes.label: "Tester"
memes.description: "Jonathan Mansel's Sealyham terrier, a loyal companion who accompanies the party from Cleveland Row to Austria, guards the camp, and provides comfort to Adèle Pleydell after her rescue."

memes.label: "The Antechamber"
memes.description: "A room in the Castle of Gath, identified by the bookseller's guide, that the rescue party uses as a waypoint during the operation to reach Adèle Pleydell."

memes.label: "The Archway"
memes.description: "A stone archway at the Castle of Gath, one of the defensive passages that Mansel must account for in planning the rescue operation."

memes.label: "The Bookseller's Guide"
memes.description: "A diagram or description of the Castle of Gath's interior provided by a bookseller in Lass, giving Mansel's rescue party critical navigational intelligence for the operation."

memes.label: "The Bridge"
memes.description: "A bridge near Poganec, identified by Jonathan Mansel as a key tactical chokepoint — a position from which a single observer with field glasses could control all movement in the valley."

memes.label: "The Cars"
memes.description: "The two Rolls-Royce motor cars that form the logistical backbone of Mansel's rescue operation, driven and maintained by Carson, Bell, and Rowley across the European continent."

memes.label: "The Cascade"
memes.description: "The waterfall or cascade that tumbles down the face of the Castle of Gath cliff, beneath which the archway door is concealed — a natural feature that masks the rescue party's hidden entrance."

memes.label: "The English Channel"
memes.description: "The English Channel, crossed by Mansel's party en route to Austria — the maritime boundary between the safety of England and the dangers of the continent."

memes.label: "The Chisel"
memes.description: "A heavy carpenter's chisel carried by Mansel as part of the rescue equipment — the tool used to force the locked doors of the Castle of Gath during the operation."

memes.label: "The Clerestory"
memes.description: "A concealed upper passage within the Castle of Gath — a clerestory running above the level of the royal apartments, used by the rescue party to move undetected."

memes.label: "The Cliff Descent Plan"
memes.description: "Mansel's contingency plan to lower Adele down the cliff face of the Castle of Gath if the rescue operation went wrong."

memes.label: "The Closet"
memes.description: "A club or public house in London where Chandos takes refuge during the operation against Rose Noble's network in Perishable Goods."

memes.label: "The Coachbuilder"
memes.description: "The London coachbuilder who works through the night to prepare the Rolls-Royces for Mansel's urgent journey to Austria, exemplifying the support network that enables the rescue mission."

memes.label: "The Cord"
memes.description: "The rope used by Mansel to lower Adèle Pleydell from the Castle of Gath terrace down the cliff face — and later cut by Rose Noble's men, trapping the rescue party inside the castle."

memes.label: The Customs Officer
memes.description: An unnamed French customs official at Dieppe who, having been bribed by Bell, expedited the clearance of Mansel's party through French Customs in under fifteen minutes, enabling their high-speed dash across Europe to Carinthia.

memes.label: "The Departure from Gath"
memes.description: "The party departs Austria. Punter and Bunch are taken away under escort. The novel's closing moments on the terrace."

memes.label: "The Dressing-Case Delivery"
memes.description: "Before departing Poganec, Mansel requests that Adèle's dressing-case be packed with her personal effects and given to the party to carry. This seemingly small request is part of Mansel's larger strategy to demonstrate to the kidnappers that the family is prepared to negotiate and deliver what they demand — buying time and establishing a channel."

memes.label: "The Drive"
memes.description: "The approach road to the Castle of Gath and the Pleydell farm. The drive serves as the setting for urgent departures, arrivals, and the novel's final exodus from Austria."

memes.label: "The Escape from Gath"
memes.description: "The team escapes from the Castle of Gath with Adèle Pleydell—a tense rope descent from the Closet windows while Hanbury's diversion draws Rose Noble's fire, cut short by Noble's final ambush."
aliases: escape from the Castle of Gath

memes.label: "The Fete"
memes.description: "A social event at White Ladies where Rose Noble, mingling among the guests, recognizes Jonathan Mansel — a moment that sets the stage for the kidnapping of Adèle Pleydell."

memes.label: "The Flower Story"
memes.description: "A fabricated tale about a chrysanthemum enthusiast named Wilberforce, deployed by Rouse as a cover story — and how Mansel sees through the deception by recognizing a borrowed human name."

memes.label: "The Frontier"
memes.description: "The Austrian frontier, crossed by Mansel's rescue party in their Rolls-Royces — a border that marks the transition into Rose Noble's territory and the domain of danger."

memes.label: "The Glove Discovery"
memes.description: "Fitch discovers one of Adèle's gloves on the road eleven miles from Poganec, not in the direction of Sava. The glove, deliberately dropped during a car transfer, provides the first physical evidence of the route by which she was taken."

memes.label: "The King"
memes.description: "The historical king associated with the Castle of Gath, whose royal apartments give the King's Closet, King's Bedchamber, and Royal Dining-Room their names, and who serves as a symbol of lost sovereignty."

memes.label: "The Lift"
memes.description: "A passenger lift or dumbwaiter in the Castle of Gath, rigged by Rose Noble's gang to transport people between floors — a mechanical trap that ensnares the rescue party during their escape."

memes.label: "The Main Gate"
memes.description: "The great main gate of the Castle of Gath — a massive entrance that swings open to allow vehicles through the courtyard, and through which Rose Noble's car races in pursuit of the escaped Adèle."

memes.label: "The Painted Face"
memes.description: "A painted face on a milestone or rock used as a covert signal by Rose Noble's gang — a mark that Mansel identifies and interprets during the search for Adèle."

memes.label: "The Pinnace"
memes.description: "A motor-boat used by George Hanbury to intercept Rose Noble's gang on the lake — a naval operation that cuts off the gang's waterborne escape route from the Castle of Gath."

memes.label: "The Porch"
memes.description: "The porch of the Castle of Gath, the scene of Casemate's hasty exit when ordered by Rose Noble to 'turn out the car' — a moment that contributes to the gang's unraveling."

memes.label: "The Observation Post"
memes.description: "The system of observation posts established by Jonathan Mansel around the Castle of Gath, enabling continuous surveillance of the fortress and its approaches."

memes.label: "The Resolution"
memes.description: "Mansel recovers. Adele and Mansel face the moral implications of their love on the terrace of the Castle of Gath."

memes.label: "The Rosary"
memes.description: "Jonathan Mansel's rosary, mentioned in the closing passages as an object of personal significance that speaks to his character beyond the adventure narrative."

memes.label: "The Safe"
memes.description: "The wall-safe in Jonathan Mansel's flat at Cleveland Row, broken open by Rose Noble's burglars who extracted its contents while leaving money and notes untouched."

memes.label: "The Sleepless Journey"
memes.description: "The punishing non-stop drive from Folkestone through France to Austria, during which Mansel's party covers hundreds of miles with minimal rest — a feat of endurance that sets the novel's pace."

memes.label: "The Southwest Tower"
memes.description: "The southwest tower of the Castle of Gath, where Adèle Pleydell is imprisoned — the specific location identified through her signals and the ultimate objective of the rescue operation."

memes.label: "The Station"
memes.description: "The railway station where Mansel's party boards the train to Innsbruck in pursuit of Rouse, and where Bell sabotages Rouse's car to delay the criminal's escape."

memes.label: "The Steps"
memes.description: "The flights of stone steps within the Castle of Gath, each terminating in a massive locked door — a defensive architecture that complicates the rescue party's approach to the tower."

memes.label: "The Telegram Arrives"
memes.description: "During the dinner at Cleveland Row on 30 August, Carson delivers a telegram from Carinthia: 'Adèle disappeared.' Mansel reveals the truth — the stolen letters were written to him by Adèle, and Rose Noble has abducted her, calling her 'perishable goods.'"

memes.label: "The Terrace"
memes.description: "The small terrace of the Castle of Gath — a balcony with a stone balustrade overlooking the cascade, where Adèle Pleydell is seen signalling and from which she is dramatically rescued."

memes.label: "The Willies"
memes.description: "The Willys-Knight motorcars used by Rose Noble's gang — powerful American automobiles that the gang relies on for transport and pursuit, and which the rescue party later commandeers."

memes.label: "Title Page"
memes.description: "Perishable Goods"

memes.label: Trapdoor
memes.description: A concealed trapdoor in the floor of the King's Closet at the [[castle-of-gath|Castle of Gath]], accessed via a steep spiral staircase in the archway, serves as the secret entry point for [[jonathan-mansel|Jonathan Mansel]]'s infiltration in Dornford Yates's *Perishable Goods* (1928).

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Uncopyright"

memes.label: "White Ladies"
memes.description: "The Pleydell family's country estate in the New Forest, Hampshire. Home to Major Pleydell, his family, and Jonathan Mansel, who resides there as a permanent guest."

memes.label: "Mr. Wilberforce"
memes.description: "A minor figure mentioned in Chapter III, whose name is used by Hannibal Rouse in a clumsy attempt at deception during the Villach episode."

memes.label: "Wiltshire"
memes.description: "The English county where William Chandos and George Hanbury first set up house together, serving as their home base before the events of Perishable Goods."

memes.label: Windlass
memes.description: A windlass mechanism at the [[castle-of-gath|Castle of Gath]] controls the great rope attached to the underside of [[the-lift|the lift]] — a concealed elevator connecting the King's Closet to the cellars — and becomes essential to [[jonathan-mansel|Jonathan Mansel]]'s operation in Dornford Yates's *Perishable Goods* (1928).
